name: Adversarial Review v1.0.8 Security Fixes
goal: Fix all 8 findings from the adversarial security review of IronClaude v1.0.8: SQL injection, path traversal, XSS, dead batch grading, asymmetric audit trail, connection leak, and two DRY violations. BUG-02 and BUG-03 land in-place (Task 4) before DRY-01 extraction (Task 7) so bug fixes survive a DRY revert.

tasks.name: SEC-01: UUID Validation in _activate_pm_remote
tasks.description: Add UUID format regex validation before the f-string SQL query in _activate_pm_remote. Validates session_uuid against ^[0-9a-fA-F]{8}-...-[0-9a-fA-F]{12}$ before constructing the SQL string. Include TODO comment documenting that this regex guard is a forced workaround for the SSH CLI interface — replace with parameterized query when run_sqlite_query supports bind params. TDD: write TestActivatePmRemote class, run RED, implement, run GREEN.

steps.description: Add TestActivatePmRemote class at end of test_orchestrator_mcp.py with two tests: rejects 36-char SQL injection UUID, accepts valid UUID format

steps.expected: FAIL: test_rejects_non_uuid_session_id fails (returns None instead of error string)

steps.description: Insert UUID regex validation in _activate_pm_remote after session_uuid is confirmed non-None and before db_path assignment (line ~1262)
steps.expected: re.fullmatch validation + early return on invalid UUID added

tasks.name: SEC-02: Path Traversal Guard in wiki_write and wiki_delete
tasks.description: Add Path.resolve().is_relative_to() guard after page_path is computed in both wiki_write and wiki_delete. Returns 'Path traversal rejected: {page}' error string if check fails. TDD: add two tests to TestWikiTools, run RED, implement, run GREEN.

steps.description: Add test_wiki_write_rejects_path_traversal and test_wiki_delete_rejects_path_traversal to TestWikiTools class
steps.expected: Two new tests written in TestWikiTools
FAIL: wiki_write creates file rather than returning error

steps.description: Add traversal guard to wiki_write: after page_path computed (~line 2466), insert if not Path(page_path).resolve().is_relative_to(Path(wiki_dir).resolve()): return f'Path traversal rejected: {page}'
Add traversal guard to wiki_delete: after page_path computed (~line 2494), insert same guard before the os.path.exists check

tasks.name: SEC-03: XSS Escaping in wiki_server.py
tasks.description: Add import html; html.escape(title) in _parse_md return; html.escape(p) in _send_404 list comprehension. New test file commander/tests/test_wiki_server.py. TDD: create test file, run RED, implement, run GREEN.

steps.description: Create commander/tests/test_wiki_server.py with test_parse_md_escapes_xss_in_title and test_parse_md_preserves_html_body
steps.expected: New test file created with sys.path insert for tools/

steps.description: Add 'import html' after 'import re' in tools/wiki_server.py
Change _parse_md return to: return html.escape(title), md.convert(body)
Update _send_404 list comprehension to use html.escape(p) for both href and link text

tasks.name: BUG-02+BUG-03: Audit Trail and Connection Safety (In-Place)
tasks.description: Fix BUG-02 (add audit_log INSERT to _activate_pm_via_sqlite) and BUG-03 (move conn=sqlite3.connect() before try, add finally: conn.close() in both methods). Fixes land in existing duplicate methods — no structural consolidation yet. Update TestActivatePmViaSqlite fixture to create audit_log table.

steps.description: Add AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA class constant to TestActivatePmViaSqlite and write test_activate_writes_audit_log
Fix _activate_pm_via_sqlite: move conn before try, add audit_log INSERT after first commit, add finally: conn.close()
Fix _deactivate_pm_via_sqlite: move conn before try, add finally: conn.close() (audit_log INSERT already present)
Run all TestActivatePmViaSqlite tests — verify GREEN
steps.expected: PASS: all tests including new ones

steps.description: Run deactivate test — verify no regression

tasks.name: DRY-01: Extract _set_pm_via_sqlite
tasks.description: Consolidate the now-fixed _activate_pm_via_sqlite and _deactivate_pm_via_sqlite into a shared _set_pm_via_sqlite(session_name, value, timeout, _claude_dir). Wrappers delegate to it. No new tests needed — existing TestActivatePmViaSqlite tests verify correctness.

steps.description: Verify baseline: all TestActivatePmViaSqlite tests pass before touching the region
Replace _activate_pm_via_sqlite and _deactivate_pm_via_sqlite (lines ~1021-1192, post-Task-4 content) with _set_pm_via_sqlite shared method plus two thin wrapper methods
Run all TestActivatePmViaSqlite tests — verify all still pass
steps.expected: PASS: behavior identical to Task 4 output

tasks.name: BUG-01: Batch Grading in _call_grader
tasks.description: Add batch=False parameter to _call_grader. When batch=True, try array extraction (re.search r'\[.*\]' with DOTALL + json.loads + validate list of grade dicts) before single-object path. Update spawn_workers call to pass batch=True. TDD: add TestCallGraderBatch class.

steps.description: Add TestCallGraderBatch class with test_batch_param_returns_list (patches secrets.token_hex, sets read_log_tail side_effect with delimiter+JSON array, asserts list returned) and test_non_batch_still_returns_dict (same setup with single JSON, no batch param, asserts dict returned)
steps.expected: New TestCallGraderBatch class with two tests
FAIL: test_batch_param_returns_list fails (TypeError: unexpected keyword argument 'batch')

steps.description: Update _call_grader signature: def _call_grader(self, system_prompt: str, user_prompt: str, batch: bool = False) -> dict | list

After post_delimiter assignment (~line 584), insert batch array extraction block: if batch: re.search r'\[.*\]' DOTALL, json.loads, validate list of grade dicts, return list if valid, otherwise fall through
steps.expected: Batch path inserted before single-object regex

steps.description: Update spawn_workers call at ~line 1510: add batch=True argument

tasks.name: DRY-02: WORKER_COMMANDS Single Definition
tasks.description: Remove duplicate WORKER_COMMANDS dict from main.py and replace with import from orchestrator_mcp. TDD: add TestWorkerCommandsImport, run RED, implement, run GREEN.

steps.description: Add TestWorkerCommandsImport class with test_worker_commands_is_same_object_in_main_and_orchestrator — imports both modules and asserts main.WORKER_COMMANDS is orchestrator_mcp.WORKER_COMMANDS

steps.expected: FAIL: main.WORKER_COMMANDS is not orchestrator_mcp.WORKER_COMMANDS (two separate dicts)

steps.description: In main.py, replace WORKER_COMMANDS = {...} dict literal (lines ~49-51) with: from ironclaude.orchestrator_mcp import WORKER_COMMANDS
